name: Puente de la Vizana y Castillo de los Pimentel — desde Alija del Infantado por la Vía de la Plata
region_intro: Hiking around Alija Del Infantado offers diverse landscapes in the province of León, Spain. The region features trails along the banks of the Eria and Órbigo rivers, providing waterside walks. Elevated routes lead to Peña Redonda and viewpoints such as El Coto and Las Suertes. The area also includes pine forests and sections of the historic Vía de la Plata. Best hiking trails around Alija Del Infantado The most popular hiking route is Basilia's Balcony and Coto, a 4.9 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 13 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views from El Coto. Another top favourite among local hikers is Banks of the Eria and Órbigo Trail, a moderate 8.3 miles (13.4 km) path. This trail follows the scenic banks of the Eria and Órbigo rivers. Local hikers also love the Peña Redonda and miradores del Coto and de las Suertes — circular by the Valles de Benavente from Morales de Rey, an 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail leading through elevated terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 43 minutes. Hiking around Alija Del Infantado is defined by riverbanks, pine forests, and elevated viewpoints. The network offers options for easy and moderate ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 10 hikers have used komoot to explore Alija Del Infantado's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Alija del Infantado?
A: There are over 10 hiking trails around Alija del Infantado, offering a variety of experiences from riverside walks to elevated viewpoints. These routes cater to both easy and moderate ability levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Alija del Infantado?
A: Yes, the region offers family-friendly options. The 'Magic Forest' route (Ruta del Bosque Mágico) is a short, imaginative 2-kilometer trail through a pine forest, adorned with handcrafted figures of goblins, fairies, and gnomes, making it a magical experience for children and adults alike.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking here?
A: Hiking around Alija del Infantado offers diverse landscapes. You can expect scenic walks along the banks of the Eria and Órbigo rivers, trails through pine forests, and routes leading to elevated viewpoints like El Coto and Las Suertes, providing panoramic views of the surrounding area.

Q: Are there any circular walks available?
A: Yes, there are several circular walks. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022509543/pena-redonda-and-miradores-del-coto-and-de-las-suertes-circular-by-the-valles-de-benavente-from-morales-de-rey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Redonda and miradores del Coto and de las Suertes — circular by the Valles de Benavente from Morales de Rey&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through elevated terrain. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1272045033/nogales-monastery-ruins-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Monastery Ruins Loop&lt;/a&gt;, offering a moderate circular experience.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see near the hiking trails?
A: Alija del Infantado is rich in history. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de los Pimentel&lt;/a&gt;, a quadrangular fortress, or the 15th-century Church of San Esteban with its Mudejar coffered ceiling. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4092040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Vizana&lt;/a&gt;, a Roman-origin bridge over the Órbigo River, is also a picturesque spot.

Q: Are there any routes that follow rivers or offer waterside views?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022487025/banks-of-the-eria-and-orbigo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Banks of the Eria and Órbigo Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice, following the scenic banks of both rivers. These routes provide tranquil waterside walks and opportunities to enjoy the natural environment.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alija del Infantado?
A: The region is suitable for hiking across various seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer provides opportunities for waterside activities, with the Puente de la Vizana becoming a fluvial beach. Even winter can be suitable, depending on conditions, for those seeking a quieter experience.

Q: Are there any trails that offer elevated views or lead to viewpoints?
A: Yes, several trails lead to elevated viewpoints. Routes to Peña Redonda and the viewpoints of El Coto and Las Suertes are available, offering expansive vistas of the surrounding landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305941795/basilia-s-balcony-and-coto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basilia's Balcony and Coto&lt;/a&gt; route specifically offers views from El Coto.

Q: Can I combine hiking with exploring the town of Alija del Infantado?
A: Yes, Alija del Infantado's historical and architectural heritage integrates well with outdoor activities. You can easily incorporate walks around the town to see sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alija del Infantado Town Center&lt;/a&gt;, the Church of San Verísimo, and the Pilgrim's Cross, providing a well-rounded experience.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Alija del Infantado?
A: Hikers using komoot often praise the varied terrain, from peaceful riverbanks to challenging elevated paths. The region's natural beauty and the opportunity to discover historical landmarks along the way are frequently highlighted as enjoyable aspects of hiking here.

Q: Are there any trails that are part of a longer, historic route?
A: Alija del Infantado lies along the historic Vía de la Plata, an ancient route that served as a Roman road and a medieval pilgrimage path. While specific komoot routes might cover sections, the presence of this ancient path suggests possibilities for longer-distance treks and historical exploration in the area.
tips:
A town in the province of León where the churches of San Verísimo and San Esteban stand out, as well as the Castillo de los Pimentel.
Also noteworthy, as a curiosity, are some small wineries nestled within the slopes of some hills.
Fortification of the town of Alija del Infantado dating from the 11th century, although it has been successively reformed by the different families to which it has belonged.

Historic bridge over the Órbigo river that connects the provinces of León and Zamora. It is part of the original layout of the Roman road, which today is the Vía de la Plata, and has been rebuilt several times over the centuries. Specifically in the War of Independence, the English destroyed the bridge to contain Napoleon's passage from Zamora. Its current form dates from the 20th century, so it has served pilgrims on the Camino de Santiago for hundreds of years.
